revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Ena-jinja]][[ja:恵那神社]] {{Expand Japanese|恵那神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Ena Shrine (Q11492453) | native_name = 恵那神社 | image = Ena Shrine.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Ena Shrine (Q11492453) | coordinates = {{coord|35.44335|137.533733}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Izanagi and Izanami|lt=Izanagi and Izanami|be|Ідзанакі і Ідзанамі|de|Izanagi und Izanami|eu|Izanagi eta Izanami|nl|Izanagi en Izanami|pl|Izanami i Izanagi|qid=Q12259961|1=Izanagi and Izanami}} | website = {{Official website|https://enajinja.jp/}} }} {{nihongo|'''Ena Shrine'''|恵那神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Mino Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|en|Shikinai Shosha|ja|式内小社|simple|Shikinai Shosha|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Q135160342|ltq=Q135160342|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}} * {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} * {{ill|Q135419779|ltq=Q135419779|qid=Q135419779|1=Shikinai Supershrine}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Mino Province|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Mino Province|ja|美濃国の式内社一覧|qid=Q11609223|1=List of Shikinaisha in Mino Province}} ** Series ordinal: 38 ** P155: {{ill|Q11364920|ltq=Q11364920|qid=Q11364920|1=Nakagawa Shrine}} ** P156: Gifu|tr|Nakatsugawa|tt|Накацугава|uk|Накацуґава|vi|Nakatsugawa, Gifu|war|Nakatsugawa, Gifu|zh_classical|中津川市|zh_min_nan|Nakatugawa-chhī|zh_yue|中津川|zh|中津川市|qid=Q844052|1=Nakatsugawa}} * {{ill|Q864156|ltq=Q864156|qid=Q864156|1=Mino Province}} * {{ill|Ena District, Gifu|lt=Ena district|ca|Ena|en|Ena District, Gifu|fa|ناحیه انا، گیفو|fr|District d'Ena|ja|恵那郡|qid=Q3030924|1=Ena District, Gifu}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|35.44335|137.533733}} == inception (P571) == == official website (P856) == * https://enajinja.jp/ ** Language of work: {{ill|Japanese ნინა|yi|יאפאניש|yo|Èdè Japaní|za|Vah Nyiedbonj|zgh|ⵜⵓⵜⵍⴰⵢⵜ ⵜⴰⵏⵉⴱⵓⵏⵜ|zh_classical|日語|zh_min_nan|Ji̍t-pún-gí|zh_yue|日文|zh|日语|zhbooks|日语|zhvoyage|日语会话手册|zu|IsiJapani|qid=Q5287|1=Japanese language}} == image (P18) == * Ena Shrine.jpg == dedicated to (P825) == * {{ill|Izanagi and Izanami|lt=Izanagi and Izanami|be|Ідзанакі і Ідзанамі|de|Izanagi und Izanami|eu|Izanagi eta Izanami|nl|Izanagi en Izanami|pl|Izanami i Izanagi|qid=Q12259961|1=Izanagi and Izanami}}https://enajinja.jp/about/ == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 181960 ** P3831: {{ill|Q135159299|ltq=Q135159299|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Q90139226|ltq=Q90139226|qid=Q90139226|1=Ken-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Shinto Directive|lt=Shinto Directive|en|Shinto Directive|fa|بخشنامه شینتو|fr|Directive shinto|id|Pengarahan Shinto|ja|神道指令|zh|神道指令|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == architectural style (P149) == * {{ill|Q210684|ltq=Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == name in kana (P1814) == * えなじんじゃ == VIAF cluster ID (P214) == * 254474094 == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 8200005008977 == NDL Authority ID (P349) == * 00633306 == Commons category (P373) == * Ena-jinja == has part(s) (P527) == * {{ill|Ena Shrine|lt=Ena Shrine|qid=Q135193982|1=Ena Shrine}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岐阜県中津川市中津川字正ヶ根3786', 'language': 'ja'} == located in/on physical feature (P706) == * {{ill|Q1339920|ltq=Q1339920|qid=Q1339920|1=Mount Ena}} {{commons category|Category:Ena-jinja}} [[JA:恵那神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11492453}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine | Name=Ena Shrine | Image=[[File:Ena Shrine.jpg|280px]]
Front shrine worship hall | Location={{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|qid=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nakatsugawa City|Q844052|qid=Q844052|1=Nakatsugawa}} Nakatsugawa Aza Shōgane 3786 (Front Shrine) | Coordinates=Front Shrine: {{Wiki coordinates 2-stage degrees minutes seconds|35|26|36.5|N|137|32|0.9|E|display=title,inline}}
Inner Shrine: {{Wiki coordinates 2-stage degrees minutes seconds|35|26|25.7|N|137|36|1.2|E|display=inline}} | Enshrined deities={{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|qid=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi}}
{{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|qid=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami}} | Shrine rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|lt=Kinpei-sha|Q119929592|qid=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}} | Established=Unknown | Main hall={{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | Annual festival={{ill|lt=September 29|Q2882|qid=Q2882|1=September 29}} (Front Shrine)
{{ill|lt=September 19|Q2896|qid=Q2896|1=September 19}} (Inner Shrine) | Rituals= | Map= {{Location map+|Japan Gifu|width=210|float=center|caption=|places= {{Location map~|Japan Gifu|lat_deg=35|lat_min=26|lat_sec=46.5|lon_deg=137|lon_min=32|lon_sec=0.9|position=left|label=Front Shrine|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{Location map~|Japan Gifu|lat_deg=35|lat_min=26|lat_sec=25.7|lon_deg=137|lon_min=36|lon_sec=1.2|position=bottom|label=Inner Shrine|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}}} {{OSM Location map|coord={{coord|35.448151|N|137.539831|E}}|fullscreen-option=0|zoom=10|float=center|width=200|caption=|title=|mark-coord1={{coord|35|26|36.5|N|137|32|0.9|E}}|label1=Front Shrine|label-pos1=left|mark-size1=8|mark-coord2={{coord|35|26|25.7|N|137|36|1.2|E}}|label2=Inner Shrine|label-pos2=right|mark-size2=8}} }} [[File:Ena Shrine Torii.jpg|thumb|180px|Torii gate]] [[File:Enajinja09.jpg|thumb|180px|Worship hall]] [[File:Shinto shrine Ena 2008-05-22.jpg|thumb|180px|Inner Shrine located at the summit area of {{ill|lt=Mount Ena|Q1339920|qid=Q1339920|1=Mount Ena}}]] '''Ena Shrine''' (えなじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Nakatsugawa City|Q844052|qid=Q844052|1=Nakatsugawa}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|qid=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. The Front Shrine is located at Shōgane, Kaore, Nakatsugawa City, and the Inner Shrine is located at the summit of Mount Ena. === Enshrined deities === === Principal deities (Front Shrine) === *{{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|qid=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi}} *{{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|qid=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami}} === Subsidiary shrine deities (Inner Shrine) === *Shinmei Shrine {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qid=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}・{{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|qid=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-hime Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime}} *Katsuragi Shrine {{ill|lt=Hitokotonushi|Q10864079|qid=Q10864079|lt=Hitokotonushi Ōmikami|1=Hitokotonushi}} *Fuji Shrine {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|Q1781862|qid=Q1781862|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime Ōmikami|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}} *Kumano Shrine {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hayatama-no-O-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} *Tsurugi Shrine {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-Kami|Q10016750|qid=Q10016750|lt=Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-Mikoto}} *Ichinomiya Shrine {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|qid=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko Ōmikami|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} === Annual festivals === *At the main shrine festival held every year on September 29, {{ill|lt=Ena Bunraku|Q11492450|qid=Q11492450|1=Ena Bunraku}} is dedicated. Ena Bunraku is designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Gifu Prefecture{{Cite web|Japanese|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/nakatugawasi/enabunnraku.html |title=Ena Bunraku |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2012-10-16}} . === History === The date of establishment is unknown. {{ill|lt=Mount Ena|Q1339920|qid=Q1339920|1=Mount Ena}} is said to be the place where {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami|qid=Q682306}} enshrined the placenta (ena, meaning umbilical cord) when she gave birth to {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|Q455602|qid=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}, and in the past Mount Ena itself was regarded as the sacred object of worship. With Mount Ena at the center, Ena Shrine lies to the west and {{ill|lt=Achi Shrine (Achi Village)|Q11657447|qid=Q11657447|lt=Achi Shrine|1=Achi Shrine (Achi Village)}} lies to the east. Later, through {{ill|lt=Shinbutsu-shūgō|Q2279088|qid=Q2279088|1=Shinbutsu-shūgō}} (the syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism), it also came to be called Ena Gongen. In the “Mino Province Jinmyōchō,” compiled after the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, seven shrines of Ena District are listed: the shrine at the summit of Mount Ena is recorded as '''Ena Shrine''', and the shrine at Kaore, Nakatsugawa City is recorded as '''Kajo Myōjin'''. At the summit of Mount Ena, the following subsidiary shrines are enshrined: Katsuragi Shrine (Hitokotonushi-no-Mikoto), Fuji Shrine (Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Kami), Kumano Shrine (Hayatama-no-O-Mikoto), Shinmei Shrine (Amaterasu Ōmikami and Toyouke Ōmikami), Tsurugi Shrine (Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-Mikoto), and Ichinomiya Shrine (Sarutahiko-no-Kami). Formerly, near the village shrine at the foot of the mountain there was also the {{ill|lt=bettō-ji|Q11396755|qid=Q11396755|1=Bettō-ji}} temple {{ill|lt=Sōsen-ji (Nakatsugawa City)|Q118391408|qid=Q118391408|lt=Sōsen-ji|1=Sōsen-ji}}, and many buildings stood there, but it is said that when {{ill|lt=Kiso Yoshimasa|Q6416834|qid=Q6416834|lt=Kiso Yoshimasa|1=Kiso Yoshimasa}}, acting on orders from {{ill|lt=Takeda Katsuyori|Q662482|qid=Q662482|1=Takeda Katsuyori}}, captured {{ill|lt=Adera Castle|Q11657387|qid=Q11657387|1=Adera Castle}} in {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|qid=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1574|Q6680|qid=Q6680|1=1574}}), the temple and buildings were destroyedMisakagoe-ki. Records remain of a ridge tag stating “Reconstruction of all seven shrines” in Tenshō 4 (1576)Nakagawa Kyūki (Hōra-zasshi ryakusō). Originally, the summit of Mount Ena was the main shrine and the foot of the mountain was the front shrine, but in {{ill|lt=Genna (Japan)|Q635245|qid=Q635245|lt=Genna|1=Genna}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1620|Q6810|qid=Q6810|1=1620}}), when the front shrine was newly rebuilt, the main shrine was changed to the inner shrine and the front shrine became the main shrine. In {{ill|lt=Meiwa|Q1146161|qid=Q1146161|1=Meiwa}} 4 ({{ill|lt=1767|Q7672|qid=Q7672|1=1767}}), the ascetic {{ill|lt=Kakumei (gyōja)|Q135984112|qid=Q135984112|lt=Kakumei|1=Kakumei}} of {{ill|lt=Shugendō|Q1483875|qid=Q1483875|1=Shugendō}} undertook a 17-day fasting practice on Mount Ena. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 4 ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=1871|1=UNKNOWN}}), it became the general tutelary shrine of {{ill|lt=Ena District|Q3030924|qid=Q3030924|1=Ena District, Gifu}} and was revered. In Meiji 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|qid=Q7757|1=1873}}), it became the village shrine of six villages in Ena District ({{ill|lt=Nakatsu Town (Gifu Prefecture)|Q11366500|qid=Q11366500|lt=Nakatsugawa Village|1=Nakatsu}}, {{ill|lt=Tegano Village|Q22125621|qid=Q22125621|1=Tegano}}, {{ill|lt=Koma-ba Village (Gifu Prefecture)|Q22126184|qid=Q22126184|lt=Koma-ba Village|1=Komanba}}, {{ill|lt=Sendanbayashi Village|Q22131707|qid=Q22131707|1=Sendanbayashi}}, {{ill|lt=Nasugawa Village|Q22126369|qid=Q22126369|1=Nasubigawa}}, {{ill|lt=Ochiai Village (Ena District, Gifu Prefecture)|Q11620598|qid=Q11620598|lt=Ochiai Village|1=Ochiai}}), and in {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|qid=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 14 ({{ill|lt=1925|Q18107|qid=Q18107|1=1925}}) it became a prefectural shrine. On March 1, {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|qid=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 28 ({{ill|lt=1953|Q18585|qid=Q18585|1=1953}}), it received designation from the Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines as a shrine designated for visits by the head of the prefectural association ({{ill|lt=Kinpei-sha|Q119929592|qid=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}}) (Designation No. Kin-21) {{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=141}}. === Designated cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property of Gifu Prefecture === *[https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/6162.html Tachi, inscription: Sadatsuna]{{Cite web|Japanese|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/kougeihin/nakatugawasi/tachimeisadatuna.html |title=Tachi, inscription: Sadatsuna |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-05-13}} Length 76.2 cm; curvature 3.6 cm; Heian period; designated on August 5, {{ill|lt=1969|Q2485|qid=Q2485|1=1969}}. The swordsmith Sadatsuna, famed for his powerful blades, is said to have lived during the {{ill|lt=Kankō|Q2736722|qid=Q2736722|1=Kankō}} era, and according to the “Ena Shrine Chronicle,” it was dedicated by {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoshinaka|Q1139202|qid=Q1139202|lt=Kiso Yoshinaka|1=Minamoto no Yoshinaka}}. It is currently preserved at the {{ill|lt=Nakatsugawa City Naegi Toyama Historical Museum|Q48747312|qid=Q48747312|1=Naegi Tōyama Historical Museum}}. === Natural Monument of Gifu Prefecture === *[https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/7393.html Married cedars of Ena Shrine] The pair of cedars in front of the main shrine were designated as a {{ill|lt=Natural Monument|Q23790|qid=Q23790|1=Natural monument}} of Gifu Prefecture on September 14, {{ill|lt=1966|Q2649|qid=Q2649|1=1966}}. The left “husband” cedar has a trunk circumference of 6.29 {{ill|lt=meters|Q11573|qid=Q11573|lt=m|1=Metre}} and a height of 47 m; the right “wife” cedar has a trunk circumference of 5.48 m and a height of 46 m. Their estimated age is 600 to 800 years, and they are in vigorous condition{{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.city.nakatsugawa.gifu.jp/wiki/%E6%81%B5%E9%82%A3%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E3%81%AE%E5%A4%AB%E5%A9%A6%E3%82%B9%E3%82%AE |title=Married Cedars of Ena Shrine |publisher=Nakatsugawa City |date=2009-01-28 |accessdate=2011-07-02}}{{Cite web|Japanese|date=|url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/tenki/nakatigawasi/enajinnjya.html|title=Married Cedars of Ena Shrine|publisher=Gifu Prefecture|accessdate=2012-10-16|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140716233352/http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/tenki/nakatigawasi/enajinnjya.html|archivedate=2014-07-16|url-status=dead|url-status-date=2017-10}}. === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Gifu Prefecture === *[https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/6809.html Ena Bunraku] Date designated: July 7, 1989 === Important Tangible Folk Cultural Property of Gifu Prefecture === *[https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/7628.html Ena Bunraku puppet heads] Date designated: December 14, 1958 === Transportation === *From Nakatsugawa Station Bus Terminal ({{ill|lt=Chūō Main Line|Q1078110|qid=Q1078110|1=Chūō Main Line}}{{ill|lt=Nakatsugawa Station|Q5359511|qid=Q5359511|1=Nakatsugawa Station}}), platform 1, take the Kawakami Line bus of {{ill|lt=Kita-Ena Kōtsū|Q11401729|qid=Q11401729|1=Kitaena Kotsu}} bound for “{{ill|lt=Mount Ena Weston Park|Q17219335|qid=Q17219335|1=Mount Ena Weston Park}},” get off at “Mount Ena Weston Park” bus stop, then walk about 45 minutes. === Chiarai Shrine === About 3.2 km southwest of Ena Shrine toward {{ill|lt=Nenoue Highlands|Q11536226|qid=Q11536226|1=Nenoue Highlands}}Nakatsugawa City, Aki 6872-1 is Chiarai Shrine. The enshrined deities are Amaterasu Ōmikami and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōyama-tsumi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, enshrined together. Originally, it stood beside the '''Chiarai Pond''', where Izanami-no-Mikoto is said to have washed and purified the placenta after giving birth to Amaterasu Ōmikami. Chiarai Pond was said to have been a large pond with a depth of 5 m and an area of 1 ha, but in {{ill|lt=Hōei|Q1145262|qid=Q1145262|1=Hōei}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1704|Q6824|qid=Q6824|1=1704}}), heavy rains caused landslides that filled it with sediment, and by the early {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|qid=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} period it had become a small pond of about one tsubo; eventually the pond disappeared, and today only a stone monument marking the site remains. In {{ill|lt=1986|Q2430|qid=Q2430|1=1986}}, during road improvements to {{ill|lt=National Route 363|Q6157348|qid=Q6157348|1=Japan National Route 363}}, part of the shrine grounds became part of the roadway, so the shrine was relocated to its present location about 260 m northeast of Chiarai Pond. It is said that after giving birth, Izanami-no-Mikoto sat on a rock to restThere is a “sitting rock” near Chiarai Pond., felt relieved, and said, “Name this place the Village of Ease.” Later, “Yasugi” changed to “Yasugi,” and in the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} period it became {{ill|lt=Agi Village|Q11657459|qid=Q11657459|lt=Agi|1=Agi}}. Near the summit area of Mount Ena is {{ill|lt=Kamisaka Pass|Q11588847|qid=Q11588847|1=Misaka Pass}}, and the origin of this place name is said to be that Izanagi-no-Mikoto and Izanami-no-Mikoto crossed the pass to enter {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|qid=Q864156|1=Mino Province}}. There is also a place name called Yufunezawa at the foot of Mount Ena, which is said to have been named because water was used there for the first bath of Amaterasu Ōmikami. === Notes === {{Notes help}} {{reflist}} === References === *“Ena Shrine Chronicle,” Kaoru Umemura, Ena Shrine Office, Meiji 45 *“History of Nakatsugawa City, Middle Volume I,” Early Modern Period (1), Chapter 4 Village Life, Section 7 Faith, 9 Ena Daigongen, pp. 827–834, Nakatsugawa City, 1988 *“History of Ena District,” Part 3 Heian Period, Chapter 12 Shrines and Buddhism, Ena Shrine / Kajo Myōjin, p. 75, Ena District Education Association, Taishō 15 *{{Cite book |Japanese |editor=Teru Kimura |title=Touring Major Shrines of Gifu Prefecture |publisher={{ill|lt=Nagara Tenjin Shrine|Q3335060|qid=Q3335060|1=Nagara Tenjin Shrine}} |date=1997 |ref={{SfnRef|Kimura|1997}}}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Ena-jinja}} * [https://enajinja.jp/ Ena Shrine] - Official website == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ena Shrine (Nakatsugawa)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ena Shrine (Nakatsugawa)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ena Shrine (Nakatsugawa)]][[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nakatsugawa]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments Designated by Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Gifu Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:山岳信仰]]
